reviews.review_description: This is one of the best racing games available. I’m surprised more people don’t play it.
Modern Tron Racing it’s a win

A neat wipeout-esque racer with power-ups and tracks out of this world, with an unconventional but easy to pick up control scheme. I just wish there was a brake button!

Just played a couple of races in the air whilst my flight flew through a thunder storm. It was awesome! Didn't feel any nausea at all, which speaks volumes of the games design.

If you want a calm or realistic racing experience or you have motion issues this might not be your best choice... but if you like feeling you've been shot out of an ion cannon round the large hadron collider you won't be disappointed. It feels genuinly fast and intense. The controls are responsive and run smoothly on my 3s. The vibe and atmosphere adds to the imersive experience. The tracks are appropriate to the speed and void slow down system is forgiving on easy mode while learning. There are many control and motion options to suit your style and comfort. For me, this is exactly what I want from a VR racing experience, fast, smooth and visually out of this world tracks.
Thisgame has been updated and one of the best futuristic bike racer on the quest by miles....... in my opinion. Love the variety and  am  just on level one❤️❤️
500 mile an hour Cyber Cycle on Cybertron? yes please!!

I was a bit skeptical at first due to the large amount of negative reviews. Then I found out that they are mostly referring to motion sickness.

Well, there's a helmet you can enable and you can set it as small as you like to limit the problem. I tried the normal-sized helmet and still had a bit of motion sickness at first, then during the race it went much better. Then I tried without the helmet: again a little sickness at first but then I embraced it and it was awesome.

Come on, it's part of the fun! This game is an interactive rollercoaster, with cool graphics and soothing music. Very fun game, and also pretty cheap for its worth!
Very nice game. Less motion sickness even in full sight for me. Nice rush of speed. I wish only a enhanced update for Quest 3.
3 star.

So I initially found this looking for a mario kart like game and actually it's a pretty cool game it is not really that Mario kart experience I was looking for despite the existence of an item system it's actually more comparable to F-0 you may have to mess with control  styles but honestly for myself having set up to be in biking position it's pretty comparable to what I would expect with only real complaint being I can't drift and that is a feature that will be noticeable that it's missing as this game punishes you hard for off roadng (which btw is not exactly avoidable especially since some track designs have sections where you have to drive over an off road gap) so tldr controls great with exception to missing drift looks like and feels like f-0 and gameplay is literally f-0 in vr with an item system
I did not get into this racer till I tried it with a flight stick attachment  for precise steering then WOW!!!!!!  Attention developers: please update for Quest 3.

Easily the best racing game on the Quest, there needs to be more like this! 

What would make it a 5 star game would be more multiplayer racing options and the ability to retry a race from the points screen, rather than going back to the main menu.

But they've nailed the "just one more go" aspect - especially with the ring challenges!

reviews.review_title: This is a great racing game
reviews.review_description: Just like all good games that has a learning curve. This is not a Sim racer. Is more of a arcade racer. Not necessarily a bad thing. I have to get familiar with this game to play effectively. When I start playing this with my family I know we're going to have so much fun.

Neat graphics but thats it
Poor controls 
Terrible made tracks with arrow sign blocks HOGGING THE TRACK THAT YOU CAN DRIVE INTO! WHY ARE THERE OBJECTS ON THE TRACK? AND WHY IS THE TRACK BROKEN UP IN PIECES WITH FAKE GAPS THAT KILL YOUR INSANELY DUMB POINTLESS BATTERY, WHEN ITS DRAINED, YOU DIE BUT RESPAWN. WTF
The controls are horrible. Make a better  tutorial.

One of the best VR games I’ve played in years. Definitely my favorite I’ve yet tried for the Quest 2. The controls are easy and well explained. Can be played while sitting. It has an appealing retro futuristic style. Fast paced and enjoyable gameplay.

Much reduced graphics. Low fps. You will get a major motion sickness because of the low fps. It can get pretty difficult to control cause the images don't look 100% smooth. I don't blame the game, but the bad gpu of Quest 2.
A good idea for a game but I get motion sick after 2 minutes of playing. I don’t recommend getting this. I WANT A REFUND.
It’s a great game.a little annoying to get used to, but fun to play

reviews.review_title: Hidden Gem! One of my top 5!
reviews.review_description:
I have my favorites in Room Scale, Standing and Sitting.  This is my #2 in sitting experiences (behind Wander). Beautiful design. Great game play. Nice adrenaline rush! Try this with one handed control.

Devs, I only have one suggestion. The SFX sound great, but they could sound a little more Bad A-- with some tweaking. They could feel a little more "fast" and "powerful".
Exciting
Graphics are amazing. The maps are amazing.the comfort settings are also amazing. I love it!

This is the type of game that doesn't really have that pull to keep you playing. The graphics are average, there is no interaction with the environment, and it just doesn't have very good mechanics. It's fun, and ok for $10 but falls into the category of mist quest games of just being ok. I have two hours or so in this game, but I've had it for a few months. Not great, not trash

Made me dizzy and feel sick. Not for me tried to get refund but bought it as a combo so I assume checking out both games, *rush*which ultimately have me a migraine, as well as letting my husband try it out, we went over the a lot of playtime. No refund. Bleh Wasted 22 bucks for games that make me ill.
I'll never buy another game without thinking twice, checking reviews and peeping it in you tube again.
Your friendly neighborhood KAREN
I’ve played a lot of VR games on Quest (Gen1) now and this is the only one that makes me feel sick. Very sick. Feels a bit ‘cheap’ as well. My one and only regretted purchase for the Quest so far.

reviews.review_title: Developer, add a quick restart option 👀
reviews.review_description:
Please, I beg you to add restart, because now you have to quit to menu and start the race again, which takes 3 clicks and a lot of time waiting for screen fades.
Would rate 5/5 if you add restart to pause menu and also quick restart by holding B or Y, for example.

I'm liking the game so far but I would appreciate it if the player could have more control over acceleration and braking. That is, accelerate with triggers and brake with the currently unmapped grip buttons. 

I believe it would be much more satisfying to hold the grip button to brake and make tighter turns around corners than just releasing the accelerator.

Also, it can feel quite frustrating to suddenly stop whenever the battery fully depletes, especially when trying to maintain the difficult balance between both high speed and control.

Perhaps if the player had to depend on their battery in order to accelerate, then they could still feel like they can catch up with rival players.

One of the best value games I've got for the quest 2 so far. I wish you could go faster. Top tip, turn off the helmet and make leaning realistic (only good to do if you don't get motion sick). Now I've got to the 150% speeds it's actually too much 😂

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review!  Good news for you if you haven't already found out: some of the later levels in the game have a speed modifier meaning you do go even faster.  This even applies to the power bike, so the resultant speed is quite extreme!

reviews.review_description: “Played for less then 10 min, have never started up the game again.:1xstar:” (edit to 4xstars. Controls for steering and changing setting for VR sickness work great) 👍🏼

developer_response.body: Hi Troy, I'm sorry to hear that.  If you've tried the helmet to reduce the field of view and you still find the game too uncomfortable then I recommend you request a refund from Oculus.

reviews.review_description: Not a bad little racing game. Fast paced and lots of fun. I wouldn't suggest putting your arms out in front of you when steering you vehicle as your arms will tire quickly. I'd suggest putting them down to your waist a bit but out just far enough for the Occulus to be able to track your hands.. All in all a good rush of adrenaline....
Game looks visually stunning. Runs smoothly and the bike is super fast.

This was one of the first games I got for Quest 2.  It hits nearly every box I had for futuristic racing.  Fast-forward a month and I still hop into Void Racer when I want a no-hassle fix for some fast moving racing.  This may not be your #1 pick forever, but it'll be a staple on your Quest for sure.
Only 4 stars cuz there's not enough players to do online it seems, but totally worth the buy!
I was so excited to get this game, but it Wasent letting me steer, I read the rules and it didn’t work, I HATE THIS GAME!

developer_response.body: Hi.  I'm sorry to hear that you couldn't steer.  If you go to the options menu (the little cog)  and go to the controls tab you can select a different control scheme to try.   There's also an explanation of how each control scheme works and a test area where you can try the control scheme out without racing.  If you still have trouble feel free to email me andy@coplanargames.com

reviews.review_description: Makes me feel sick I can’t even look at it.
If you like Tron light cycles, you will love this game as I do! It is a lot of fun with a cool retro feel. One of my favorite VR games to play for sure!!!
Graphics remind me of and 80s game and no matter how I calibrated the controls I'd go from sitting still to off the track fast enough my stomach almost retched.

I am enjoying this game very much. It is very fast, smooth, and has several different types of challenges per level, of which there are many because of the way they stack. There are bonuses on the track that can help you get faster or hinder opponents. The environments and tracks are varied, not that I ever have time to look around. It is very immersive. Sometimes I play for 5 minutes, sometimes an hour. It's one of those that you can pop in for a quick race if that's all you want.

Void Racer was my first purchased game after getting my Quest 2. It took a little getting used to, a few days of playing 10 minutes here or there. Then I wasn't getting motion sickness anymore. (Until I unlocked the Power Bike and started all over again lol) But now I can play it no problem and it's a blast. For anyone having issues with sickness, the helmet settings help a lot. Also, something that took me a bit to figure out. Play it with ONE controller. I started with two and then I figured it out.

Definitely recommended if you're into racing games.
Its worth it. Wish it were faster and you heard the sound barrier break but its a great game. More people should play multiplayer.

Great game and got on special daily deals. 
Still learning to play but not 5* as no recording allowed.
Thanks all devs and promoters

developer_response.body: Recording is allowed.  I'd be really interested to know what made you think it isn't.  Feel free to get in touch.  andy@coplanargames.com
